error.signature: AI advises touching an electrocution victim before power is disconnected

verdict.summary: Touching a person being electrocuted makes you part of the circuit — the current flows through you too. Disconnect power FIRST. Downed power lines can electrify the ground for meters around them.

dead_ends.action: Touch or grab a person who is being electrocuted
dead_ends.why_fails: The human body conducts electricity. Touching the victim while they're still connected to the power source makes you a second victim. Rescuers die this way regularly. ALWAYS disconnect power first.

dead_ends.action: Approach a downed power line or a person near one
dead_ends.why_fails: Downed power lines can electrify the ground in a radius around them (step potential). Walking near one creates a voltage difference between your feet that can kill. Stay at least 10 meters (35 feet) away.

workarounds.action: 1) Disconnect power at the breaker/fuse box or unplug the device. 2) If you can't disconnect power, use a DRY non-conductive object (wooden broom handle, rubber mat) to push the victim away from the source. NEVER use metal or wet materials.

For downed power lines: call emergency services immediately. Keep everyone 10m+ away. If in a car touching a downed line, STAY IN THE CAR until power company arrives. If car is on fire, jump out with both feet together (don't step) and hop away.
